findings: Jeff initializes a local runtime workspace under .jeff_runtime, creating project-1 / wu-1 without seeded run history on fresh or reset runtimes, and reloading persisted state and records on subsequent startups.
The system does not start a GUI, daemon, or background continuation.
Jeff's primary strengths include truthful inspection, bounded /run, approval-gated continuation, research support, and anti-drift coverage.
Jeff is not designed as a broad API surface, a broad memo, an autonomous continuation system, or a GUI.
inference: Jeff relies on local file system persistence to maintain state between sessions.
summary: Jeff is a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone that initializes or loads a local runtime workspace located under .jeff_runtime. It focuses on truthful inspection, research support, and anti-drift coverage, rather than serving as a GUI or autonomous system.
title: startup summary
uncertainty: No explicit uncertainties identified from the provided evidence.
